NixOS Discourse
Virtually mounting Google Drive
Help

Log In

​

​
Virtually mounting Google Drive
Help

1.6k
views

1
link




Jun 2024
19d ago

StrongSand94191

1
Jun 2024
Hello! I would like to virtually mount 2 Google Drive accounts in Dolphin. I don’t want them to use up storage space on my computer, so I don’t want offline sync, just to be able to access them from Dolphin.

Previously, I used the Online Accounts menu in System Settings (in Kubuntu) to link my Google account, but I can’t find the option in NixOS.

Is this possible on NixOS (24.05)?

Thanks in advance!



1.6k
views

1
link





mausch
Jun 2024
No idea about Dolphin specifically but FWIW I use and recommend https://rclone.org/ for mounting Google Drive



StrongSand94191
Jun 2024
Mounting Google Drive with Rclone works, but whenever I try to open it with Dolphin, it just loads infinitely.


27 days later

oldhomemovie
Jun 2024
Were you able to battle this issue, by any chance? If so, how did you do it? :thinking:



StrongSand94191
Jul 2024
I was not able to solve this :sweat_smile:


2 years later

DarkestDot
19d
Set up rclone and then
set up a service for rclone in your home-manager config

systemd.user.services.rclone-gdrive = {
    Unit = {
      Description = "RClone Mount for Google Drive";
      After = [ "network-online.target" ];
      Wants = [ "network-online.target" ];
    };
    Service = {
      ExecStartPre = "${pkgs.coreutils}/bin/mkdir -p %h/GDrive";
      ExecStart = "${pkgs.rclone}/bin/rclone mount rclonemountnameforgdrive: %h/GDrive --vfs-cache-mode full";
      ExecStop = "${pkgs.fuse}/bin/fusermount -u %h/GDrive";
      Restart = "on-failure";
      RestartSec = "10s";
      Environment = [ "PATH=/run/wrappers/bin:$PATH" ];
    };
    Install = {
      WantedBy = [ "default.target" ];
    };
  };

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env sh
mkdir -p ~/.local/share/tmux/panes
mkdir -p ~/.local/share/tmux/archive
LOG=~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
PANE_DIR=~/.local/share/tmux/panes
ARCHIVE_DIR=~/.local/share/tmux/archive

while true; do
  sleep 30

  # Append window metadata
  tmux list-windows -a \
    -F "$(date +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S)  #{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}  #{pane_current_path}" \
    >>"$LOG" 2>/dev/null

  # Rotate existing live snapshots to .old
  for f in "$PANE_DIR"/*.txt; do
    [ -f "$f" ] && mv "$f" "${f%.txt}.old"
  done

  # Recapture all currently live panes
  tmux list-panes -a -F "#{session_name} #{window_index} #{pane_index} #{pane_id}" \
    2>/dev/null | while IFS= read -r line; do
    sess=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f1)
    widx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f2)
    pidx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f3)
    pane_id=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f4)
    tmux capture-pane -pt "$pane_id" -S - 2>/dev/null \
      >"$PANE_DIR/$sess--$widx--$pidx.txt"
  done

  # For each .old: if a live .txt exists → pane survived → delete .old
  #                if no live .txt  → pane closed  → archive with timestamp
  ts=$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
  for old in "$PANE_DIR"/*.old; do
    [ -f "$old" ] || continue
    base=$(basename "${old%.old}")
    if [ -f "$PANE_DIR/$base.txt" ]; then
      rm -f "$old"
    else
      mv "$old" "$ARCHIVE_DIR/${base}--${ts}.txt"
    fi
  done
done

content.content:
1→# From: https://github.com/caarlos0/dotfiles/blob/f9d985c3e8817f8fca133d908a910f173ea933d6/modules/tmux/tmux.conf
     2→# License: MIT
     3→
     4→# Color Mode
     5→set -g default-terminal "screen-256color"
     6→setenv -g COLORTERM "truecolor"
     7→
     8→set -s escape-time 0
     9→setw -g mouse on
    10→
    11→# Fix SSH agent after reconnecting
    12→# https://blog.testdouble.com/posts/2016-11-18-reconciling-tmux-and-ssh-agent-forwarding/
    13→set -g update-environment "DISPLAY SSH_ASKPASS SSH_AGENT_PID SSH_CONNECTION WINDOWID XAUTHORITY WAYLAND_DISPLAY SWAYSOCK"
    14→setenv -g SSH_AUTH_SOCK $HOME/.ssh/ssh_auth_sock
    15→
    16→# Ensure window index numbers get reordered on delete.
    17→set-option -g renumber-windows on
    18→
    19→# Auto rename tmux window to current cwd
    20→set-option -g status-interval 1
    21→set-option -g automatic-rename on
    22→set-option -g automatic-rename-format '#{b:pane_current_path}'
    23→
    24→# Vim-mode copy/paste
    25→set-window-option -g mode-keys vi
    26→bind-key -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection
    27→bind-key -T copy-mode-vi r send-keys -X rectangle-toggle
    28→bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'
    29→
    30→# Open splits and windows in the current folder
    31→bind u split-window -l 30% -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
    32→bind i split-window -l 40% -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
    33→bind c new-window -c "#{pane_current_path}"
    34→
    35→# Repeat time
    36→set-option -g repeat-time 1000
    37→
    38→# Clipboard
    39→set -g set-clipboard on
    40→
    41→# Status bar
    42→set -g status "on"
    43→set -g status-justify "left"
    44→set -g status-left-length "100"
    45→set -g status-right-length "100"
    46→set -g status-left ""
    47→set -g status-left-style NONE
    48→set -g status-right-style NONE
    49→
    50→# Reload tmux config
    51→bind t source-file ~/.config/tmux/tmux.conf
    52→
    53→# Default shell
    54→set -gu default-command
    55→set -g default-shell "$SHELL"
    56→
    57→# Rename session and window
    58→bind r command-prompt -I "#{window_name}" "rename-window '%%'"
    59→bind R command-prompt -I "#{session_name}" "rename-session '%%'"
    60→
    61→# Copy text on select
    62→bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "reattach-to-user-namespace pbcopy"
    63→
    64→# Switch window
    65→bind , previous-window
    66→bind . next-window
    67→
    68→# Move pane bindings
    69→bind-key h   select-pane -L
    70→bind-key j   select-pane -D
    71→bind-key k   select-pane -U
    72→bind-key l   select-pane -R
    73→
    74→# Swap window bindings
    75→bind-key -r "<" swap-window -d -t -1

file.content:
# From: https://github.com/caarlos0/dotfiles/blob/f9d985c3e8817f8fca133d908a910f173ea933d6/modules/tmux/tmux.conf
# License: MIT

# Color Mode
set -g default-terminal "screen-256color"
setenv -g COLORTERM "truecolor"

set -s escape-time 0
setw -g mouse on

# Fix SSH agent after reconnecting
# https://blog.testdouble.com/posts/2016-11-18-reconciling-tmux-and-ssh-agent-forwarding/
set -g update-environment "DISPLAY SSH_ASKPASS SSH_AGENT_PID SSH_CONNECTION WINDOWID XAUTHORITY WAYLAND_DISPLAY SWAYSOCK"
setenv -g SSH_AUTH_SOCK $HOME/.ssh/ssh_auth_sock

# Ensure window index numbers get reordered on delete.
set-option -g renumber-windows on

# Auto rename tmux window to current cwd
set-option -g status-interval 1
set-option -g automatic-rename on
set-option -g automatic-rename-format '#{b:pane_current_path}'

# Vim-mode copy/paste
set-window-option -g mode-keys vi
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi r send-keys -X rectangle-toggle
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'

# Open splits and windows in the current folder
bind u split-window -l 30% -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind i split-window -l 40% -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind c new-window -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Repeat time
set-option -g repeat-time 1000

# Clipboard
set -g set-clipboard on

# Status bar
set -g status "on"
set -g status-justify "left"
set -g status-left-length "100"
set -g status-right-length "100"
set -g status-left ""
set -g status-left-style NONE
set -g status-right-style NONE

# Reload tmux config
bind t source-file ~/.config/tmux/tmux.conf

# Default shell
set -gu default-command
set -g default-shell "$SHELL"

# Rename session and window
bind r command-prompt -I "#{window_name}" "rename-window '%%'"
bind R command-prompt -I "#{session_name}" "rename-session '%%'"

# Copy text on select
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "reattach-to-user-namespace pbcopy"

# Switch window
bind , previous-window
bind . next-window

# Move pane bindings
bind-key h   select-pane -L
bind-key j   select-pane -D
bind-key k   select-pane -U
bind-key l   select-pane -R

# Swap window bindings
bind-key -r "<" swap-window -d -t -1
bind-key -r ">" swap-window -d -t +1

# Resize pane bindings
bind-key -r H resize-pane -L 5
bind-key -r J resize-pane -D 5
bind-key -r K resize-pane -U 5
bind-key -r L resize-pane -R 5

# Split pane bindings
bind-key "|" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "\\" split-window -fh -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "-" split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "_" split-window -fv -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "%" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key '"' split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Yank pane history to clipboard
bind y run-shell 'tmux capture-pane -pS - | pbcopy && tmux display-message "Full pane history copied to clipboard"'
bind v run-shell 'tmux capture-pane -p | pbcopy && tmux display-message "Visible pane copied to clipboard"'

# Pane number indicator
set -g display-panes-colour colour233
set -g display-panes-active-colour colour245

# Clock mode
set -g clock-mode-colour colour39
set -g clock-mode-style 24

# =============================================================================
# Status bar — clean green theme
# =============================================================================
set -g status-style "bg=colour22,fg=colour255"
set -g status-left "#[bg=colour28,fg=colour255,bold] #S #[bg=colour22,fg=colour28] "
set -g status-right "#[fg=colour250]#{?#{pane_current_command},#{pane_current_command},} #[fg=colour28]| #[fg=colour255]%Y/%m/%d %H:%M:%S "
set -g status-left-length 30
set -g status-right-length 60

# Window status
set -g window-status-format " #I:#W "
set -g window-status-current-format "#[bg=colour28,fg=colour255,bold] #I:#W "
set -g window-status-separator ""

# Pane border
set -g pane-border-style "fg=colour238"
set -g pane-active-border-style "fg=colour82,bold"

# Message style
set -g message-style "bg=colour28,fg=colour255"

# =============================================================================
# Plugin configuration
# =============================================================================
# Resurrect + Continuum (session persistence)
set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'
set -g @continuum-restore 'on'
set -g @continuum-save-interval '15'

# Tmux-thumbs (quick text copy)
set -g @thumbs-key Space

# Sessionx (session manager)
set -g @sessionx-bind 'o'

# Tmux sessionizer (fzf session picker)
bind S run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tss_function'"

# Last session (quick flip)
bind Tab switch-client -l

# Cross-session window picker
bind W run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tsw_function'"

# Pane content search (live + archived)
bind H run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tsh_function'"

# Kill sessions/windows via fzf
bind K run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tsk_function'"

# Extrakto (text extraction)
set -g @extrakto_key 'tab'

set -g history-limit 0

# Persistent session history logger (starts once per server boot)
run-shell "pgrep -f 'session-logger.sh' > /dev/null 2>&1 || (nohup ~/.config/tmux/session-logger.sh > /dev/null 2>&1 &)"

content.thinking:
Now I understand the tmux setup. The session-logger.sh script:
1. Logs session/window metadata every 30 seconds to `~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log`
2. Captures pane contents to `~/.local/share/tmux/panes/*.txt`
3. Archives closed panes to `~/.local/share/tmux/archive/*.txt`

The user wants to **backup** these local tmux session logs. I need to clarify what "backup" means - this could mean:
1. Sync to a remote location (cloud storage, another machine)
2. Create periodic local backups/snapshots


3. Set up a systemd service to regularly copy/sync the logs somewhere

The first message about mounting Google Drive and this request about backing up tmux logs might be connected—they could want to sync the logs to Google Drive. But I'm not certain if these are related tasks or separate ones, so I should ask for clarification about what destination they have in mind and how frequently they want backups to run.

Looking at their existing services like dotfiles-updater and code-syncer, I can follow a similar pattern for the backup mechanism. Let me check what backup infrastructure they already have in place to understand the conventions they're using.
